description: Materials: These socks are made from organic materials, including 35% wool, 33% polyester, and 30% cotton. Warmth: They are designed to retain heat very well and release moisture, making them suitable for keeping feet warm during the winter months. They are claimed to be four times warmer than ordinary cotton socks. Comfort: The socks offer extra softness and comfort with a thickened and looped terry, which helps reduce foot friction and relieve foot fatigue. Versatility: They can be worn in both formal and informal settings, including offices, parties, hiking, trekking, football, golf, cricket, cycling, and more. Cold Resistance: The gentle welt and looped terry are designed to enhance comfort and protect the feet in cold conditions, claiming resistance to temperatures as low as -25°C. Moisture Management: The socks have natural moisture-wicking ability, which helps remove moisture from the feet, keeping them fresher and cooler for extended periods without emitting bad odors. Durability: They feature a reinforced heel and toe, ensuring long-lasting comfort . Tested Standards: These socks have been tested to BS4745 standards, indicating their quality. Insulation: They have a TOG rating of 2.4, providing extra insulation against low temperatures. These socks seem to offer a combination of warmth, comfort, and durability, making them suitable for various settings and especially beneficial for individuals with diabetes who need to keep their feet warm and dry to maintain good foot health
